input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use MSBuild's `IsTestProject` property, which is automatically set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package. Wrap the MTP settings in a conditional `PropertyGroup`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- only for test projects (set automatically by Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk) -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** Any project that references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` gets `IsTestProject=true` injected by the SDK. Your library projects never reference that package, so they're unaffected.

**Fallback alternative:** If for some reason `IsTestProject` isn't set early enough (rare), you can condition on a naming convention instead:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is preferred because it's convention-independent and works with any test project name.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a straightforward MSBuild knowledge question. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or has a naming convention. But the most robust approach for MTP migration is to condition on the presence of `IsTestProject` or use a naming convention.

Actually, the cleanest and most standard way is:


I can use a condition that checks if `IsTestProject` is set to true — MSBuild automatically sets this when a project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. Alternatively, I could rely on a naming convention like checking if the project name ends with `.Tests`.
